title: Principal Software Engineer
summary: Principal engineer and technical lead across CMMI product systems, appraisal tooling, agentic AI review platforms, Kentico/Xperience delivery, Azure-hosted application infrastructure, and Ukrainian/Eastern Europe-based offshore delivery coordination.
highlights: Led MAGI, an internal FastAPI platform for advisory, explainable, read-only analysis over CMMI Appraisal System data using Azure SQL schema discovery, Azure DevOps source context, Azure OpenAI, SQL validation, and bounded execution.
Built MAGI deployment architecture for Azure App Service, Azure SQL, Azure AI Search, Azure OpenAI/APIM, Key Vault, ACR, managed identity, SAML/Kentico role access, and Azure DevOps pipelines.

Developed the CMMI Model Viewer stack with .NET 10 API/domain services, React 19/Vite SPA, SAML integration, Azure Blob media storage, Key Vault-backed secrets, Terraform-managed Azure infrastructure, and environment-specific deployment workflows.

Modernized the CMMI Website on Kentico Xperience 13/.NET 8 with shared Kentico customizations, generated content models, custom listing widgets, TOTP/QR utilities, Survey Tool API integration, Cypress coverage, and Azure slot/webjob deployment automation.

Maintained and improved cross-system engineering surfaces across appraisal-system, cmmi-identity, core-api, course-roster, cmmi-cca, Stamper, CmmiWebsite, MAGI, and cmmi-model-viewer, including code coverage, package updates, deployment fixes, and identity integration work.
Served as mentor and technical lead for 4-5 engineers while coordinating internal engineers and Ukrainian/Eastern Europe-based offshore teams across delivery, review, and agile ceremonies.

title: Senior Software Engineer
summary: Senior full-stack engineer on CMMI appraisal, course, website, and data systems across .NET, SQL Server, JavaScript, Azure, Docker, and CI/CD workflows.
highlights: Built and maintained the CMMI Appraisal System across internal, external, and public web surfaces, including ASP.NET Core services, SQL Server-backed distributed cache, HTML email workflows, webpack-built front ends, Cypress coverage, and Dockerized local development.
Worked on appraisal workflow coverage for benchmark, evaluation, and sustainment appraisal paths across internal reviewer, external user, and public-facing application surfaces.
Delivered Course Roster functionality with EF migrations, API/web separation, webpack front-end builds, environment-specific CMMI base URLs, and Cypress integration tests.

Worked across supporting CMMI data and integration services including kentico-salesforce-functions, AssociateExamsIngest, LitmosConnectAssociateExams, V2-Appraisal-Marks-Stand-Alone, V2UpgradeTrainingIngest, core-api, and cmmi-identity.
Designed and implemented data transition strategies from multiple older CMMI platforms into newer systems.
Pioneered Go-based microservice architecture work and contributed to the CMMI Institute website rollout on Kentico.

title: Co-Owner
summary: Co-owner and software lead across Swim View, pool-deck data visualization, mobile software, BLE firmware, cloud services, ecommerce systems, and Mexican engineering collaboration.
highlights: Led a three-engineer effort with Mexican engineering collaboration to build Swim View, a .NET, C#, React, and Redux application that visualizes data gathered from swimming pool decks via Swim Tower products.
Developed BLE accessory firmware in C, iPhone and iPad applications, and an App Engine backend.
Authored a custom Go ecommerce platform integrating Stripe and UPS, including UPS API integration and a 3D bin-packing heuristic.
Published public supporting artifacts around 3D bin packing, Arduino/Nordic board recovery, quadrature decoder testing, and ecommerce ecosystem research.
Designed high-precision clock synchronization logic for BLE device alignment using drift and offset bounding.
Built iOS data visualization tooling for a swimming smart meter using d3.js.

title: Software Engineer
summary: Maintained and optimized in-house .NET C# MVC applications and supported web development delivery.
highlights: Inherited and improved a slow .NET C# MVC application for Service Link call-center operations.
Maintained internal MVC systems and supported the broader web development team.

title: System Analyst III
summary: Modernized circulation reporting and contributed to CMS-backed mobile and desktop publishing systems.
highlights: Revamped PG Delivery, Dispatch, and Depot circulation reporting from VAX/VMS into a C# and AngularJS system spanning thousands of pages.
Supported printing and delivery workflows for newspapers including the Post-Gazette, New York Times, and Wall Street Journal across Pennsylvania.
Published the truckexport reporting-engine work as a concrete example of the C# circulation reporting modernization path.
Built a C# integration to move photos from Photoshelter into the Libercus CMS.
Helped overhaul mobile and desktop websites into the Libercus CMS and maintained Joomla-based sports writer blogs.

title: Development Team Lead
summary: Technical lead across touch panels, client websites, mobile platforms, hosting, and campaign systems.
highlights: Built applications with Objective-C, Flash, HTML5, jQuery, PHP, CSS, and C#.
Managed hosting and web server configuration across Linux, Windows, macOS, and AWS.
Contributed to Transitions Healthy Insights, Transitions SolFX, and MCG Jazz.

title: Development Team Lead
summary: Led development across brand websites, campaign microsites, Flash, PHP, C#, JavaScript, and hosting infrastructure.
highlights: Developed an interactive Heinz.com front page using Papervision 3D.
Configured Linux, Windows, macOS, and AWS hosting environments.
Delivered work for Heinz.com, American Eagle Outfitters, Art Institutes, Paramount on Lake Eola, Transitions.com, and SolFX.com.

title: Developer
summary: Built internationalized interactive web work for the Bratz dolls website.
highlights: Developed the Bratz interactive website across a dozen languages.

title: Software Engineer
summary: Worked on the Netspoke web conferencing product.
highlights: Developed and maintained existing web conferencing product functionality.

title: Developer
summary: Built training websites using streaming video and web technologies.
highlights: Developed training websites with HTML, CSS, JavaScript, PHP, and streaming video.
Supported projects including Heinz Ketchup University, Aptech, NAFCU Services Corporation, US Filter, and Invivodata.

title: Developer
highlights: Built web and kiosk presentation work for Visa and Sheetz.

Built UNIX security training simulations for CMU Software Engineering Institute.
Worked on projects for Oakley, CCAC, FrameworksIT, PA Cleanways, Ford Properties, City Charter High School, and Pittsburgh Tissue Engineering Initiative.
